IWT Long Calls.note: No entries triggered in this period — IVR condition rarely met

market_context.regime: Strong bull year (SPY +32%). VIX ranged 13-31. IVR averaged 25% (low IV buyer's market).
market_context.key_event: April 2025 tariff shock: VIX spiked to 31, SPY briefly dropped before massive recovery.
market_context.implication: Low IV throughout most of year meant IWT income strategies had fewer quality entry signals. Long directional positioning (buy-and-hold) was optimal in this environment.

honest_findings.why: SPY +32% is exceptional (avg year ~8-10%). All active strategies use position sizing that limits exposure, protecting downside but capping upside vs pure buy-and-hold.
honest_findings.income_strategy_context: Credit spreads produced 69% win rate with controlled drawdown (-0.4%). In a normal year (8-10% SPY), income vs buy-and-hold comparison would be much closer.
honest_findings.position_sizing_impact: Conservative Kelly/2-5% risk limits intentionally protect capital. Not designed to beat exceptional bull years.

data_provenance.options_pnl: SYNTHETIC — BSM with real historical VIX as IV proxy. Not actual market option prices. Actual fills would differ due to bid/ask spread, volatility skew, and term structure.
data_provenance.equity_pnl: REAL — actual price changes with $0.01/share slippage
data_provenance.disclaimer: Past backtest results do not guarantee future performance. This is for educational purposes only.
